stories.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
stories.factualSummary: TechCrunch reports that OpenAI introduced GPT-5.6 in three variants, positioning the family for coding, cybersecurity, scientific research, and general AI workloads.
takeaways: The family uses three performance and cost tiers rather than a single flagship configuration.
OpenAI is emphasizing coding efficiency and defensive cybersecurity capabilities in the release.

stories.title: Merck and Mastercard are seeing real agentic AI results. Both say the plumbing came first.
stories.factualSummary: VentureBeat describes how Merck and Mastercard are applying AI agents to regulated workflows while prioritizing shared infrastructure, governed context, permissions, and human oversight.
takeaways: Reusable infrastructure and access controls are presented as prerequisites for scaling agents safely.
Both organizations keep humans in governing roles where errors carry regulatory or trust consequences.

stories.title: IShowSpeed to lead first-ever YouTube FIFA Creator Cup
stories.factualSummary: The official YouTube Blog says the Creator Cup will stream from Central Park on July 12 at 5:00 p.m. ET through FIFA's official YouTube channel and IShowSpeed's channel.
takeaways: The event combines creators, football figures, and a global livestream around the World Cup.
YouTube is using creator-led live programming to expand its sports coverage.
